rationale: Both mentions refer to the same statistical method (Maximum Likelihood Estimation) with identical type (Method) and consistent definitions across lectures.

evidence_spans.span: Maximum-Likelihood Estimation estimates an econometric model by maximizing the likelihood of the observed sample. The likelihood is viewed as a function of the model parameters with the sample held fixed.

Maximum Likelihood Estimation selects parameter values that maximize the likelihood. The likelihood compares parameter values after the observed data are held fixed. An MLE estimator may be written as
